channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Opinion: Lessons from a bad weather forecast
item.description: Residents in and around Washington braced themselves for damaging storms earlier this week, but turns out it was a forecast flop. One local meteorologist apologized.
item.content:encoded:
Residents in and around Washington braced themselves for damaging storms earlier this week, but turns out it was a forecast flop. One local meteorologist apologized.

(Image credit: Andy Newman/ASSOCIATED PRESS)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: When health insurance costs $2,500 per month, families make tough choices
item.description: A self-employed couple already had to dip into retirement savings for health costs. Now, they are skipping vacations and canceling streaming to afford health insurance.
item.content:encoded:
A self-employed couple already had to dip into retirement savings for health costs. Now, they are skipping vacations and canceling streaming to afford health insurance.

(Image credit: Jarod Lew for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Meet the Dutch art detective who tracks down stolen masterpieces
item.description: For 20 years, Dutch art detective Arthur Brand has acted as an intermediary between the police and people who know where stolen artwork might be hiding. He says patience and trust are everything.
item.content:encoded: For 20 years, Dutch art detective Arthur Brand has acted as an intermediary between the police and people who know where stolen artwork might be hiding. He says patience and trust are everything.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Rosman

item.title: DHS shutdown hurts families' access to detention facilities, Democrat says
item.description: The difficulties for families adds to the patchwork of complaints about immigration oversight and other issues while the department remains without government funding for five weeks.
item.content:encoded:
The difficulties for families adds to the patchwork of complaints about immigration oversight and other issues while the department remains without government funding for five weeks.

(Image credit: Andrew Caballero-Reynolds)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Iran war enters its fourth week with no clear end in sight
item.description: As the war in the Middle East enters its fourth week, President Trump says the U.S. is considering "winding down" military efforts, as it also seeks to ease the energy crisis by lifting sanctions on Iranian oil stranded at sea.
item.content:encoded:
As the war in the Middle East enters its fourth week, President Trump says the U.S. is considering "winding down" military efforts, as it also seeks to ease the energy crisis by lifting sanctions on Iranian oil stranded at sea.

(Image credit: Amir Levy)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: U.S. judge rules against Pentagon restrictions on press coverage
item.description: The policy required media organizations to pledge not to gather information unless Defense officials formally authorized its release. A U.S. judge said the rules are at odds with the First Amendment.
item.content:encoded:
The policy required media organizations to pledge not to gather information unless Defense officials formally authorized its release. A U.S. judge said the rules are at odds with the First Amendment.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow

item.title: Jury finds Elon Musk misled investors during Twitter purchase
item.description: A jury has found Elon Musk liable for misleading investors by deliberately driving down Twitter's stock price in the tumultuous months leading up to his 2022 acquisition of the social media company for $44 billion. But it absolved him of some fraud allegations.
item.content:encoded:
A jury has found Elon Musk liable for misleading investors by deliberately driving down Twitter's stock price in the tumultuous months leading up to his 2022 acquisition of the social media company for $44 billion. But it absolved him of some fraud allegations.

(Image credit: Markus Schreiber)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Over 5,500 told to evacuate flooding in Hawaii as officials warn that dam could fail
item.description: Muddy floodwaters from severe rains have inundated communities and prompted evacuation orders for more than 5,500 people in towns north of Honolulu. Officials are warning about the possible failure of a 120-year-old dam.
item.content:encoded:
Muddy floodwaters from severe rains have inundated communities and prompted evacuation orders for more than 5,500 people in towns north of Honolulu. Officials are warning about the possible failure of a 120-year-old dam.

(Image credit: Mengshin Lin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: CBS News shutters its storied radio news service after nearly a century, ending an era
item.description: The change is part of a round of layoffs at CBS News. When the radio service began operation in September 1927, it was a precursor to the entire CBS network. Today its top-of-the-hour news roundups are delivered to about 700 stations across the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
The change is part of a round of layoffs at CBS News. When the radio service began operation in September 1927, it was a precursor to the entire CBS network. Today its top-of-the-hour news roundups are delivered to about 700 stations across the U.S.

(Image credit: GRS)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Federal prosecutors ask to dismiss charges against officers in Breonna Taylor raid
item.description: Feds move to dismiss charges against officers accused of falsifying warrant in Breonna Taylor raid.
item.content:encoded:
Feds move to dismiss charges against officers accused of falsifying warrant in Breonna Taylor raid.

(Image credit: Amy Harris/Invision/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
